content.problemStatement:
Today is Chef's birthday! Chef bakes KKK different cakes, numbered from 111 to KKK, and calls his friends over for his birthday party.

The birthday party is scheduled to happen for a total of NNN minutes. In the iii-th minute of the birthday party, the following happens:


If the AiA_iAi​-th cake is still uneaten, Chef's friends eat the AiA_iAi​-th cake
Otherwise, they do nothing
However, Chef wants some cakes left for himself at the end of the birthday party, so he has decided to use his superpowers. Chef has MMM superpowers of the form [Li,Ri][L_i,R_i][Li​,Ri​]. Chef can do the following operation **exactly once** during the birthday party:


Pick time ttt (1≤t≤N)(1 \leq t \leq N)(1≤t≤N)
Pick superpower iii (1≤i≤M)(1 \leq i \leq M)(1≤i≤M)
At the **start** of the ttt-th minute of the party, refill all cakes numbered from LiL_iLi​ to RiR_iRi​.

Note that cakes are refilled before Chef's friends attempt to eat a cake in that minute.
Let CCC denote the number of cakes remaining at the end of the birthday party if Chef applies the operation at the **start** of the ttt-th minute. Chef's happiness is defined to be (N−t+1)⋅C(N-t+1) \cdot C(N−t+1)⋅C.

Find the **maximum** possible happiness that Chef can achieve if he performs the operation optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK — the duration of the party, the number of superpowers that Chef has and the number of cakes that Chef bakes.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers — the elements of the array AAA.
The next MMM lines describe Chef's superpowers. The iii-th of these MMM lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​ — the iii-th superpower that Chef has.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible happiness that Chef can achieve if he performs the operation optimally.
constraints: The sum of NNN, the sum of MMM and the sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Using the 222nd superpower which is [2,4][2,4][2,4] at the start of the 333rd minute gives a happiness of (6−3+1)⋅2=8(6-3+1)\cdot2 = 8(6−3+1)⋅2=8. A visualization of the party can be seen in the gif below.
